stance: HOLD — premium-priced execution, watch FY27 royalty mix
summary: Arm Holdings is the world's most-licensed CPU IP company. Revenue splits between upfront licenses (lumpy, large contracts) and recurring royalties (per-unit on shipped chips). ARM revenue compounded from $939M (FY25-Q1) to $1.49B (FY26-Q4) — a +59% cumulative lift over 7 quarters — driven by ARMv9 royalty rate (~2x v8) and accelerating data-center / AI design wins (Nvidia Grace, AWS Graviton, Microsoft Cobalt). Operating leverage on a stable R&D base (~$2.5B/yr) is the main earnings driver.
quotes: The mix shift toward ARMv9 continues to outpace our expectations, and the data-center pipeline now represents over 25% of new licensing revenue.
We're guiding Q4 revenue of $1.43 to $1.47 billion, the mid-point reflects continued royalty momentum and meaningful license commitment from automotive customers.
bull: ARMv9 royalty migration: ~2x royalty rate vs ARMv8, with adoption curve still in early innings — multi-year tailwind.
Data-center design wins (Nvidia Grace, AWS Graviton, Microsoft Cobalt, Google Axion) shift mix from low-ASP mobile to high-ASP server CPUs.
Operating leverage: R&D held at ~$2.5B/yr against revenue growing +20% YoY → operating margin expanded from 21.5% (FY25-Q1) to 35.1% (FY26-Q4).
Automotive licensing growing rapidly with multi-year contract structure; the FY26 licensing pipeline is the strongest on record.
FCF generation accelerating: gross margin 95%+ structural, capex minimal.
bear: Trading at ~105x P/E FY1, ~82x EV/EBITDA FY1 — premium ~115% to peer median (~49x).
Multiple expansion thesis is exhausted; further appreciation must come from EPS revisions higher.
Smartphone unit volume mature; royalty growth depends on ARMv9 ASP, not unit growth.
China revenue (~25%) exposed to export-control escalation.
Licensing revenue is lumpy quarter-to-quarter; one delayed deal can reset trajectory.
trigger_up: FY27 royalty per-unit ASP > $0.20 (vs ~$0.16 today) confirms ARMv9 mix shift.
New hyperscaler design win (Meta, Tencent) announced.
License commitments from a new automotive OEM partner.
trigger_down: Royalty per-unit ASP plateaus below $0.18 in any FY27 quarter.
China revenue declines >5% sequentially on export-control news.
Smartphone royalty unit count declines YoY for two consecutive quarters.
bottom_line: HOLD. ARM is executing — eight straight quarters with revenue at or above guidance midpoint, and operating margin has nearly doubled in seven quarters. The bull case is structurally intact: ARMv9 mix, data-center pipeline, automotive licensing. The bear case is purely valuation: at 105x forward P/E, the price assumes consensus EPS is materially too low. A position size should reflect that the upside requires EPS-revision tailwinds beyond what's currently modeled.
disclaimer: All multiples sourced from FactSet Workstation captured 5/15/26. Fundamentals from ARM 20-F (FY2025) + ARM 6-K filings (FY26 Q1-Q4).
